name: tracerelay
description: Self-evolving schema runtime plugin for research, analysis, structured profiling, prior-work continuation, change inspection, and gap-driven next-step planning in local LLM workflows.

interface.shortDescription: Research, memory recall, schema evolution, and lineage tracing for long-running local LLM workflows
interface.longDescription: Run TraceRelay inside Codex as a repo-local plugin. The plugin connects to the running docker-compose MCP server, exposes high-level tools for structuring research, continuing earlier work, inspecting changes, analyzing gaps, and planning next-step search, and keeps schema lineage inspectable across Web, MCP, and the database.
defaultPrompt: Use continue_prior_work when the user wants to keep going on the same subject, reuse previous findings, or avoid starting from scratch
Use structure_subject when the user wants to research, analyze, profile, organize, or map a subject into structured fields
Use inspect_latest_changes when the user asks what changed, what happened in the latest run, why it retried, or why the schema evolved
Use plan_next_step before generic search so TraceRelay decides what is missing and what to do next
Use prepare_search_queries when external search is needed so the query is grounded in known facts and open gaps
